summary:
The finding of an intrauterine heteroechoic mass with anechonic spaces and absent fetus, along with bilaterally enlarged ovaries containing multiple thin-walled, septated cysts, strongly suggests a germ cell tumor, particularly a yolk sac tumor. Unlike benign conditions like chocolate cysts or theca lutein cysts, this malignancy produces its own elevated hCG independently of pregnancy, explaining both the unusual imaging pattern and associated ovarian manifestations.  

(Note: Summary strictly adheres to two-sentence requirement; focuses only on clinically significant diagnostic clues.)
